_metadata.description: Physics constants for the 8 planets and Earth's Moon

fields.auroraColors: Dominant visible aurora hues based on atmospheric composition

planets.name: Mercury
planets.auroraNote: Weak magnetosphere drives localized polar X-ray/UV aurora; blue tinge in upper exosphere

planets.name: Venus
planets.auroraNote: Oxygen-driven 557.7 nm green emission detected by Venus Express in upper atmosphere

planets.name: Earth
planets.auroraNote: Oxygen (557.7 nm green / 630 nm red) and nitrogen (purple/blue) dominate visible aurora

planets.name: Moon
planets.auroraNote: No global magnetic field and negligible atmosphere; no sustained aurora observed

planets.name: Mars
planets.auroraNote: CO₂/N₂ thin atmosphere produces UV aurora; crustal magnetic patches cause faint visible blue glow

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.auroraNote: H₃⁺ ions in hydrogen/helium atmosphere generate brilliant pink/violet aurora; Io plasma torus amplifies intensity

planets.name: Saturn
planets.auroraNote: Hydrogen atmosphere produces UV/blue aurora; ring plasma adds violet tones around polar ovals

planets.name: Uranus
planets.auroraNote: Hydrogen-dominated atmosphere with tilted magnetic axis produces diffuse blue auroral patches

planets.name: Neptune
planets.auroraNote: H₂/He atmosphere with irregular magnetic field generates blue-violet aurora across broad latitude bands
